medications.indication_rationale: First-line for tinea pedis; apply to affected areas and the whole sole twice daily for ~2 weeks (continue after clearing). Source used terbinafine 1% cream.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to affected areas + soles twice daily for ~2 weeks (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: dilute antiseptic foot soaks (e.g. povidone-iodine or aluminium acetate)
medications.indication_rationale: Dries macerated web spaces and helps fissures; source advised twice-daily 5-minute soaks.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 5-minute soaks twice daily to macerated areas (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Powder clumps in fissures and irritates — use antiseptic drying/antifungal instead.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Dry meticulously between the toes after washing, wear cotton socks (change when damp), rotate footwear (don't wear the same shoes two days running), use a separate foot towel, and wear thongs in communal showers.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Moisture control removes the fungal-friendly environment and prevents reinfection.

patient_education_points.point: This is athlete's foot (a fungal infection) that thrives in warm, damp feet. An antifungal cream clears it, and antiseptic soaks dry out the soggy, cracked skin between your toes. Keep going for a week or so after it looks clear.
Dry carefully between the toes, wear cotton socks, rotate your shoes so they dry out, use a separate foot towel, and wear thongs in gym showers.

Go to ED / see a doctor urgently if the redness spreads up the top of your foot or leg as a hot painful patch, you get a red streak, the fissures leak thick yellow pus, or you develop a fever — the cracks can let bacteria in (especially important if you ever develop diabetes).

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Fissure healing; secondary infection; nail involvement.

must_include_items: Topical antifungal + antiseptic soaks for macerated/fissured skin
Moisture/footwear control (dry between toes, cotton socks, rotate shoes)
Secondary-cellulitis safety-net (spreading redness, streaking, pus, fever) — fissures are entry points
acceptable_alternatives: Terbinafine or an azole; aluminium-acetate/povidone soaks
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires topical antifungal + moisture control and a secondary-cellulitis safety-net.
